title: arabis alpina subsp. cantabrica
intro: Arabis alpina subsp. cantabrica, also known as Cantabrica Rock-cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers and is typically found in mountainous regions of northern Spain and southern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as isothiocyanates and flavonoids. Arabis alpina subsp. cantabrica has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions, and its leaves and flowers can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
